rulings.Case Title: VASILIKI SERVETA VS. KEVIN J. HESLIN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 13, 2022 line 1. DEFENDANT ELSIE MAXWELL ROSENTHAL, AN INDIVIDUAL AND TRUSTEE, MARK FLETCHER, NEW MILLENNIUM HOUNDS LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is CONTINUED to May 23, 2022 on the Court's own motion. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TRUE GOSPEL MISSIONARY BAPTIST CHURCH VS. TY INVESTMENT LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 13, 2022 line 2. Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction is GRANTED. Defendant TY Investment LLC and their foreclosure trustee, agents, servants, and employees, are enjoined from conducting any sale, conveyance of the real property commonly known as 106 Broad Street, San Francisco, California [hereinafter known as the property] or taking any action to sell, liquidate, convey or heyptheticate or any action other than to main the status quo of the property. No opposition filed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CARITAS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION VS. DALIA VILCHEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to ANSWER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 13, 2022 line 3. PLAINTIFF CARITAS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, FPI MANAGEMENT INC., MISSION HOUSING DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION DEMURRER to ANSWER to Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED, in part and OVERRULED in part. No opposition filed. 5 days leave to amend. The demurrer is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND as to affirmative defenses 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 10, 11, 14, and 15 for Defendant to allege facts that support their legal conclusions. The demurrer is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND as to affirmative defenses 12, 13, 16, and 17, as they are not legally cognizable affirmative defenses to unlawful detainer. The demurrer is OVERRULED as to affirmative defense 9, as it sufficiently alleges an affirmative defense to unlawful detainer. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JOHN BRITTON VS. MARIO URIBE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant [Ccp Sec 1170.8, 2023.010, 2030.290, 2031.300]
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 13, 2022 line 4. PLAINTIFF JOHN BRITTON Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant [Ccp Sec 1170.8, 2023.010, 2030.290, 2031.300] is GRANTED, subject to opposition. Defendant shall provide complete, verified responses without objections to the Requests for Production, Special Interrogatories, and Form Interrogatories (general) within 5 days of notice of entry of this order. Sanctions granted in the amount of $300. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: ROY LEE VS. MICHAEL GONZALES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Defendant, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant [Ccp Sec 1170.8, 2023.010, 2030.290, 2031.300, 2033.280(B) And (C)]
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 13, 2022 line 5.
